To find the current wind speed in London (or any other location) you can check one of the following reliable sources:

| Service | How to use |
|--------|------------|
| **Weather.com** (The Weather Channel) | Go to the site or app, type “London, UK” in the search bar. The main dashboard shows wind speed, direction, and gusts. |
| **BBC Weather** | Visit the BBC Weather page, enter “London” or choose “London Central.” It lists wind details in the “Wind” section. |
| **OpenWeatherMap API** | If you’re comfortable with APIs, you can get up‑to‑date data in JSON: `https://api.openweathermap.org/data/2.5/weather?q=London,UK&appid=YOUR_API_KEY`. The `wind` field contains speed (m/s) and direction. |
| **AccuWeather** | Search “London” and view the “Wind” column in the brief forecast. |
| **Weather Underground** | Provides a “Wind” tab that shows speed, direction, gusts, and a 24‑hour trend. |

These platforms pull data directly from local weather stations, satellites, and official meteorological services, so they’ll give you the most accurate and up‑to‑date wind speed (typically shown in km/h or mph, depending on the site’s settings).<|return|>
